executiveSummary.text: T-Mobile has the most compelling combination of postpaid subscriber growth, free cash flow generation and 5G monetisation optionality among US telecoms, and the shares are undervalued relative to peers at 20x forward P/E — BUY is the weighted recommendation.

businessModel.text: T-Mobile US is the third-largest US mobile carrier by subscriber count, with approximately 130 million total customers and approximately 100 million postpaid subscribers following the integration of the Sprint network. The company has a distinct competitive positioning as the 'uncarrier' — historically differentiated through no-contract pricing and aggressive promotional strategies — and has been the primary beneficiary of customer switching among the three national carriers over the past decade. T-Mobile's 5G network advantage (built on 600 MHz and 2.5 GHz spectrum holdings from the Sprint acquisition) underpins both mobile and fixed wireless services. The company generates revenues from postpaid phone plans, prepaid brands (Metro by T-Mobile, Mint Mobile), home broadband (FWA) and business enterprise services.

recentCatalysts.text: T-Mobile's postpaid phone subscriber growth has consistently exceeded AT&T and Verizon, making it the primary switching destination for quality-conscious mobile consumers. The 5G FWA product (home internet) is gaining traction as a low-cost alternative to wired broadband in suburban and rural markets — a market that Verizon and AT&T are struggling to compete in at scale. The Sprint merger synergies, now fully integrated, provide an earnings cushion that has enabled the company to maintain industry-leading EBITDA margins. Management has committed to returning $22.5bn+ to shareholders via buybacks through 2027.

thesisEvaluation.text:
Bull Case (81): Postpaid phone subscriber additions remain the highest in the industry. 5G fixed wireless access (FWA) grows into a meaningful revenue stream. Metro by T-Mobile (prepaid) expansion adds incremental subscribers. Free cash flow exceeds $18bn annually, funding aggressive buybacks. EPS grows 10–12% annually. Price: $230+.
            Base Case (50): Postpaid net adds remain positive but decelerate to industry-average levels. FWA adoption grows steadily. 5G network investments plateau, improving free cash flow margins. EPS grows at 7–9% annually. Price: $190–$215.
            Bear Case (40): Intense price competition from AT&T and Verizon erodes postpaid ARPUs. FWA growth stalls. T-Mobile's postpaid phone churn rises as promotional pricing expires. Merger synergies from Sprint prove more difficult to extract than modelled. EPS growth falls to 3–5%. Price: below $165.
            Weighted Score: 81 — Bull 81 × 30% + Base 50 × 50% + Bear 40 × 20%

bull.scenario: Bull Case (81): Postpaid phone subscriber additions remain the highest in the industry. 5G fixed wireless access (FWA) grows into a meaningful revenue stream. Metro by T-Mobile (prepaid) expansion adds incremental subscribers. Free cash flow exceeds $18bn annually, funding aggressive buybacks. EPS grows 10–12% annually. Price: $230+.

base.scenario: Base Case (50): Postpaid net adds remain positive but decelerate to industry-average levels. FWA adoption grows steadily. 5G network investments plateau, improving free cash flow margins. EPS grows at 7–9% annually. Price: $190–$215.

bear.scenario: Bear Case (40): Intense price competition from AT&T and Verizon erodes postpaid ARPUs. FWA growth stalls. T-Mobile's postpaid phone churn rises as promotional pricing expires. Merger synergies from Sprint prove more difficult to extract than modelled. EPS growth falls to 3–5%. Price: below $165.

keyRisks.text: Competitive response: AT&T and Verizon have significantly increased promotional activity. If sustained, this could pressure T-Mobile's ARPUs and increase churn as existing customers are targeted by co

risks.risk: Competitive response: AT&T and Verizon have significantly increased promotional activity. If sustained, this could pressure T-Mobile's ARPUs and increase churn as existing customers are targeted by competitive offers.

Postpaid subscriber ceiling: T-Mobile has taken significant market share from peers. As the addressable market of switchers shrinks, maintaining above-peer net additions requires increasingly competitive offers that may erode returns.

Regulatory and merger risk: The telecommunications sector is subject to ongoing regulatory scrutiny (FCC, state AGs). Any future acquisition or spectrum consolidation faces significant regulatory barriers.

Handset subsidy and financing costs: T-Mobile's device financing (JUMP! and similar programmes) and promotional handset subsidies affect free cash flow in ways that are sensitive to device upgrade cycles and consumer finance conditions.

5G FWA execution risk: Fixed wireless access faces physical limitations (building penetration, geographic coverage) that constrain the total addressable market. The thesis depends on continued customer acquisition in this product category.

whoShouldOwn.text: Owners: Total return investors seeking ahigh-quality telecommunications franchise with industry-leading growth and FCF generation. Also suited to investors who want 5G infrastructure exposure without the binary risk of pure-play tower companies. Avoiders: Yield-focused investors seeking telecom-sector income — T-Mobile does not pay a dividend. Investors with low risk tolerance or high portfolio weight in telecom should also exercise caution.

recommendation.text: BUY. BUY below $155 (approximately 16x forward P/E — below peer group average and below T-Mobile's own historical range). Reduce above $215 (approximately 22x forward P/E — fully values the FCF generation and merger synergy extraction but leaves little upside).

entryExit.text:
T-Mobile US Inc (TMUS) — Entry / Exit Framework

Accumulate: $172.22–$185.92 — pullback zone within the 52-week range. The stock is attractively priced relative to the recent move.
Stop-loss: $172.29 — a close below the 52-week low would signal fundamental deterioration.
Target 1: $227.77 — mean reversion toward the midpoint of the 52-week range. Near-term catalyst needed to confirm momentum.
Target 2: $281.36 — above the 52-week high on sustained volume. Only appropriate for position-initiation with conviction.
